ocr.text: In the context of Emerson Electric's ongoing commitment to optimizing operational efficiency within its industrial
manufacturing sector, the Lean manufacturing metrics data table provides a comprehensive overview of key performance
indicators over the past two years. The table highlights significant improvements in several areas that are critical for assessing
the effectiveness of Lean initiatives. For instance, the total output for 2019 was recorded at 1,200 units, which laid the
groundwork for a substantial increase to 1,300 units in 2020, marking a growth rate of approximately 25%. This upward trend
in production is indicative of the successful implementation of Lean principles aimed at reducing waste and enhancing value
creation across the organization.
LeanManufacturingMetrics  Year  Revenue(€M)  NetIncome(€M)  CAPEX(€M)  OPEX(€M)  GrossMargin(%)
180  1,000  [HIDDEN]  5-YearCAGR  12.5%  20%  [HIDDEN]  18%  30%
Total  [HIDDEN]  1,080  700  4,500  [HIDDEN]  ConsistentGrowth  HighEfficiency  ProfitabilityImprovement
Furthermore, the metrics reveal that the average cycle time decreased from 150 minutes in 2019 to an improved figure,
demonstrating a commitment to refining processes and reducing time-to-market. Notably, the total operational costs were
reduced from 7,300 EUR in 2019 to a more efficient figure, reflecting the organization's dedication to cost management while

maintaining output levels. 

Additionally, the data table emphasizes the importance of quality control as a pivotal aspect of Lean
manufacturing. The percentage of defective products, which was 16.2% in 2019, has been a key focus area for continuous
improvement initiatives, and subsequent efforts have led to a reduction in this metric, highlighting the effectiveness of quality
management strategies. The report also illustrates the substantial reduction in waste, where the waste metrics fell from 250 units
to a significantly lower value, underscoring the effectiveness of process optimization techniques employed. Moreover, the
average lead time, which was recorded at 12.5 days in 2019, has been targeted for further reduction, and preliminary results
suggest a promising trajectory towards achieving a target of 15.0 days. This improvement not only enhances customer

satisfaction through quicker delivery times but also contributes to a more agile and responsive manufacturing environment. 

The
strategic emphasis on Lean methodologies at Emerson Electric is further evidenced by the continuous monitoring and
evaluation of these metrics, which are integral to the organization’s pursuit of operational excellence. As we move forward into
2021 and beyond, the insights derived from these metrics will inform future initiatives aimed at driving further enhancements
across the manufacturing landscape. By maintaining a sharp focus on these critical performance indicators, Emerson Electric is
well-positioned to leverage Lean principles for sustained growth and competitive advantage in the European industrial sector.
